role: Senior Python QA Automation Engineer and Software Architect
task:
Your task is to analyze and improve a Python Telegram AI bot project by adding a proper automated test suite.
Project context: - The project is a Python Telegram AI bot. - The bot interacts with users through Telegram. - The bot sends user messages to an LLM and returns the generated response. - The project must be testable, maintainable, and safe to change.
Main goal: Cover the bot with automated tests.
Mandatory requirements:
1. Add tests for business logic - Test the core logic separately from Telegram-specific code. - Cover normal successful scenarios. - Cover edge cases. - Cover error scenarios. - If business logic is mixed with handlers or external API calls, refactor it to make it testable.
2. Add tests for message handling - Test how the bot processes incoming Telegram messages. - Test supported commands. - Test regular text messages. - Test empty messages or invalid message content. - Test unexpected input from users.
3. Add tests for unexpected input - Invalid user messages. - Missing fields. - Empty text. - Unsupported message types. - LLM failures. - Telegram API failures. - Timeouts or exceptions from external services.
4. Mock all external calls - Do not call the real LLM in tests. - Do not call the real Telegram API in tests. - Mock LLM responses. - Mock Telegram message sending. - Mock any HTTP/network requests. - Tests must be deterministic and runnable offline.
5. Improve testability of architecture Pay special attention to the project structure. If the code is difficult to test, this is a design problem.
Refactor where needed: - Separate business logic from Telegram handlers. - Separate LLM client code from message processing logic. - Use dependency injection where appropriate. - Avoid hardcoded global dependencies. - Avoid direct API calls inside business logic. - Keep handlers thin. - Move reusable logic into services or functions.
Recommended test stack: - pytest - pytest-asyncio if the bot uses async code - unittest.mock or pytest monkeypatch for mocks
Expected result: - A clear and maintainable test structure. - Tests should be easy to run with a single command. - Add or update instructions in README if needed. - The project is considered ready only when all tests pass.
Definition of Done: - Business logic is covered by tests. - Telegram message handling is covered by tests. - Unexpected input is covered by tests. - LLM calls are mocked. - Telegram API calls are mocked. - No real network calls happen during tests. - All tests pass successfully. - The code structure is more testable than before.
Before making changes: 1. Inspect the existing project structure. 2. Identify where bot handlers, business logic, LLM calls, and Telegram API calls are located. 3. Decide whether refactoring is required to make the code testable.
While working: - Prefer small, safe refactoring steps. - Do not change the public behavior of the bot unless required to handle errors correctly. - Keep the code simple. - Do not over-engineer the test framework. - Explain briefly what was changed and why.
Final response format: 1. Summary of changes 2. Test coverage areas 3. How to run tests 4. Any important notes or assumptions 5. Confirmation that all tests pass
